rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF EDUARDA O. BUTAC
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/18/2024. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) A copy of the remote appearance information required by LRSF 14.1(C)(6) must be attached to and served with the DE-120 form at least 15 days before the hearing. 3) The zip code for Arnold and Clarita Olorvida listed on the proof of service attached to the DE-120 form on file appears incorrect. 4) Notice must be given to the successors of the postdeceased beneficiaries who have not signed a power of attorney authorizing Kemp & Associates to receive notice, as the powers of attorney executed by the postdeceased beneficiaries are no longer effective. 5) An original small estate affidavit (13100 declaration) signed by all the successors of Azuceno Olorvida must be filed with a certified copy of his death certificate. The affidavit must be filed conventionally with a pleading cover sheet attached (cannot be e-filed). LRSF 14.59(C)(2). 6) No bank statements have been filed to verify the ending cash balance of each account. LRSF 14.34(D). A declaration re retention of original statements required by LRSF 14.59(A) must also be filed. 7) No prior court approval has been obtained to retain the real estate attorney and make payment from the estate. 8) Are the services performed by California Inheritance Group ordinary services that should have been performed by the administrator? If so, should CIG's 10% fee paid from the estate be deducted from the administrator's statutory compensation? 9) The schedule of proposed distribution must include a specific amount of net cash distributable to each distributee, assignee, and alternate distributee required to be identified by Probate Code section 11603(c). The proposed order must include the same descriptions. 10) There is only one witness to the powers of attorney purportedly signed by Steven Poblete, Teove Poblete, and Edlen Alayon. Direct notice must be given to them if the powers of attorney are invalid. 11) There are no signatures of the witnesses to the power of attorney purportedly signed by Victor Beltran. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 12) The power of attorney purportedly signed by Arnold Olorvida with his fingerprint was not dated. Direct notice must be given to him if the power of attorney is invalid. 13) There are no assignments signed by Richmond Poblete, Salome Olorvida, Cristy Amoro, Allan A.S. Olorvida, Daniel Olorvida, Gliza Olorvida, Franz Olorvida, Artemio loysaynon, Agapito Losaynon, and Artemio Losaynon. Note: No reserve for closing expenses has been requested.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MOJO TUBUTU
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) When an accounting is presented, original bank or brokerage statements must be filed to verify the ending balance(s) shown in the accounting. None appears to have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. (3) A copy of the seller's closing statement must be filed to verify the costs of sale. (4) "E-filing fee" alone is not an adequate explanation (description) of costs. Attorney costs do not show WHAT was e-filed to incur any of the given costs listed, so that, other than the costs that correspond to actual dates when papers were filed, it is impossible to determine if costs are accurate. E-filing costs should also have been $5.00 for e-filed document, not $11.50 or $38.50. Was one of the two entries on 6/23/23 a duplicate? The filing fee for a petition for final distribution is $435.00. (5) The attorney's fee declaration appears to be missing a page. Only the time entries for the sale of the real property (4.6 hours) is attached. (The calculation of the fees as shown on page 2 of the fee declaration also do not appear to be correct.) Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The I&A value of the estate was $110,000. The property was sold for $85,000, or a loss of $25,000. [Probate Code Section 10800(b).] The calculation of the statutory fee (paragraph 23) is not exactly accurate and also is not supported by the accounting schedules. The receipts were also a little higher than shown in paragraph 23. In any event, the "bottom line" is close enough that this does not need to be amended, but Counsel should take note of this. Christopher D. Jew also filed a Request for Special Notice on 10/25/19 and never withdrew it.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERALD R. BARRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Satisfaction Of Debt And Determination Of Expenses Of Administration Related To Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, despite the following issues that remain unaddressed: 1) There is no proof of service on the Marin County Tax Collector and brokers entitled to commissions from the sale proceeds. They must be served in the manner required by CCP sections 413.10 et seq. (applicable to service of a civil summons) at least 30 days before the hearing. Probate Code section 10361.6(a)(2) & (3). The 30-day period cannot be shortened by the Court. Section 10361.6(c). 2) The lien holders have not signed consents to execution of a release and deed of reconveyance to evidence their agreement to allocate the sale proceeds as proposed by the petition. The petition requests an order determining how much each lien holder should receive from the sale proceeds and compelling each lien holder to execute a release and deed of reconveyance by accepting that amount. Absent the lien holders' signed consents, is the relief against the lienholders sought by this petition beyond the scope of section 10361.5 (which only refers to an "order determining the amount of expenses of administration that are reasonably related to the administration of that encumbered property as provided in section 11420(a)(1)")? 3) There is no proof of service on the beneficiary of the devisee trust. Notice must be given to the trust beneficicary at least 15 days before the hearing, absent a waiver (which can be signed by the petitioner in her capacity as the beneficiary's POA agent). Sections 10361.6(b)(3) & 1208(b). The 15-day period cannot be shortened by the Court. Section 10361.6(c). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF NANCY NGAI YING CHEW
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/25/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: 1. Contrary to what Counsel stated at the hearing on 12/18/23, an amended petition was not filed that morning, just a "Supplemental Declaration." 2. The executor makes various allegations in his "Supplemental Declaration" regarding the purported actual amount transferred from the estate of Frank Chew and, contrary to the order for final distribution filed on 4/21/21, which showed just cash in a checking and a savings account, now alleges that assets transferred were "a mixture of credits and cash." 3. The order for final distribution in Frank Chew's estate showed $1,000.15 in the checking account and, after $9,960.21 in fees and costs to the attorney, $283,785.53 in the savings account, which total $284,785.68, not $284,765.89. 4. This petition still has not been properly verified by the executor. Since no amended petition was filed, his verification of the "supplemental declaration" is not a verification of his petition. (The pleading filed on 8/24/20 was his so-called "Amendment to Petition for Probate.") 5. Since the "Supplemental Declaration" alleges facts materially different from the facts alleged in the petition, notice with a copy of the "Supplemental Declaration" must be given to the trustee. 6. The trustee, if it is, in fact, James Chew, after all, has not waived an accounting by the executor, and Ming Yu Wu's waiver of accounting attached to his (not her?) petition was apparently fallacious. Deadline is 3/15/24 for filing responsive papers (an amended petition for final distribution with an accounting if the accounting is not waived, Corrected I&As, if appropriate, and proof of service of the amended petition) and providing courtesy copies to the assigned examiner. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MAUREEN E. CROWE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/8/24 in light of the "Update" filed on 2/13/24 regarding a possible change in counsel for the executor. NB: The primary reason for the continuance from 11/8/23 to 12/20/23 was the appraisal of non-cash assets by the executor, not the probate referee, and, regardless of who had access to a decedent's assets immediately following the date of death until a personal representative was appointed, the I&A must show the value of assets as of the date of death, which is why a personal representative's accounting must begin on the date of death. If she is unable to obtain bank records, etc., in order to account for activity that preceded her appointment, she should address this in her accounting. Notice was also defective. The "Update" was apparently emailed to Mr. Madden only, not to the interested parties. If neither a Corrected I&A nor a Substitution of Attorney is filed by 3/29/24, an appearance will likely be required on 4/8/24.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MEN CHEW GEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. APPEARANCE FOR NO RESPONSE: If a hearing is continued for issues raised by an examiner to be addressed and there is no response prior to the continued hearing date, the matter is automatically continued again, usually for one month. If there is still no response, an appearance is required. If no one appears after the automatic second continuance, the matter is ordinarily taken off calendar if it is not a compulsory petition, or an Order to Appear is issued. Issues posted in the tentative ruling for the hearing on 10/9/23 have not yet been addressed. Please have the CASE NUMBER and the CASE NAME ready for the clerk. Parties appearing must check in for the hearing using their FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear in person or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. Please review LRSF 14.1 D. for information on remote appearances. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 160 225 4765 and password is 514879. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DANNY SIU-CHAN YAN AND BOONRAT A. YAN REVOCABLE TRUST dated February 12, 2001
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Removing Trustee, Appointing Interim Trustee Pending Trial, And Appointing Successor; For Surcharge And Other Relief; For Double
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JIAJU LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/25/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The proof of publication shows that this is the estate of Charles Si Yan Zhou. Notice must be published, correctly identifying this decedent. (2) A Judicial Council form must not be used for a supplement to a pleading. [CRC 7.104(b)(2).] A supplement must be prepared on pleading paper and must be signed and verified by the petitioner who is or seeks to be appointed as a fiduciary. Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/15/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PAUL D. BRIARD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 3/18/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (2) When second generation or more distant heirs are listed, the deceased ancestor through whom they take (or would have taken) shall be named and his or her date of death and relationship to the decedent shall be stated. [LRSF 14.18 C.] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 3/8/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
